answer:
The longer piece must be at least 7 meters (half the wire) and at most 14 meters.  
For the square’s area to exceed 4, its side must be more than 2, so the perimeter (the longer piece) must be more than 8 meters.  
The favorable range for the cut is from 6 to 14 meters for the longer piece, but since the longer piece is at least 7, the favorable length is from 8 to 14, giving a probability of (14−8)/14 = 6/14 = 3/7, which matches option B when simplified.

\boxed{B}
